operating_state: Total revenue increased 7% to $1.1 billion above prior year driven by 8% capacity expansion on strong operational performance. Passenger revenue grew 5% from robust travel demand particularly for premium products, partially offset by yield compression. Operating income declined $224 million to $2.3 billion despite revenue growth.
cost_structure: Total operating expense increased 10% or $1.3 billion primarily from higher employee costs due to wage increases, elevated fuel expense, and capacity-related cost expansion. Cost per available seat mile increased 2% while non-fuel unit costs rose 0.6% driven by wage inflation. Operating margin compression occurred as expense growth outpaced revenue expansion.
material_events: Board elected Maria Black and Willie CW Chiang as directors effective April 26, 2024. Annual shareholder meeting on June 20, 2024 elected all twelve director nominees and approved advisory executive compensation vote. Shareholders ratified Ernst & Young LLP as 2024 independent auditors while rejecting proposals on political contribution reporting and non-interference policy adoption.
macro_exposures: No material changes from risk factors described in Form 10-K were disclosed during the quarter.
forward_outlook: Management guidance and strategic direction were not specified in the available filing excerpts.
uncertainty_profile: No new material contingencies, regulatory matters, or accounting standard changes were disclosed beyond standard risk factors previously reported.
